words.definition: a document showing that you have paid for something
example_sentences: Always keep your receipt in case you need to return the item.
She lost the receipt and therefore couldn't get a refund.
The receipt shows the exact amount and date of purchase.
words.pronunciation_tip: rih-SEET — two syllables, stress on second. The 'p' is completely silent.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'the paper that shows I paid' — say 'the receipt'. Remember: silent P in receipt.
words.collocations: ['keep the receipt', 'show your receipt', 'ask for a receipt']

words.definition: a reduction in the normal price of something
example_sentences: Students receive a generous 10% discount at this museum.
They offered a big discount on all last season's models.
I can discount the price slightly for a long-term regular customer.
words.pronunciation_tip: DIS-kownt — stress on first syllable.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'reduction in price' every time — say 'discount'.

words.definition: money paid back when you return something or are unsatisfied
example_sentences: I asked for a full refund on the faulty laptop.
They refused to give me a refund without the original receipt.
The company promptly issued a full refund.
words.pronunciation_tip: REE-fund — stress on first syllable.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'give me back my money' formally — say 'I'd like a refund please'.

words.definition: a plan for how to spend money within certain limits
example_sentences: We're on a very tight budget this month unfortunately.
I carefully budgeted for the holiday months in advance.
Try hard to stay within your budget.
words.pronunciation_tip: BUJ-it — two syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'I plan my money' — say 'I budget carefully' or 'I'm on a budget'.

words.definition: something bought for much less than its usual price
example_sentences: This jacket was an absolute bargain — it was half price!
She always bargains at the souk market.
I absolutely love finding a real bargain in the January sales.
words.pronunciation_tip: BAR-gun — two syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'a cheap thing I bought' — say 'I got a bargain' or 'it was a real bargain'.

words.definition: something bought suddenly without any prior planning
example_sentences: That expensive jacket was an impulse buy — I didn't really need it.
I tend to make impulse buys when I'm bored and browsing online.
She regretted the impulse buy the very next morning.
words.pronunciation_tip: IM-pulss-by.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'I bought it without thinking' — say 'it was an impulse buy'.
words.collocations: ['make an impulse buy', 'typical impulse buy', 'regret impulse buy']

words.definition: an occasion when goods or services are bought or sold
example_sentences: The entire transaction was completed in just a few seconds.
The bank records every single transaction on your account.
He made several large and suspicious transactions last month.
words.pronunciation_tip: tran-ZAK-shun — three syllables, stress on second.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'buying and selling' — say 'transaction' in formal or financial contexts.

words.definition: a manufacturer's promise to repair or replace a faulty product
example_sentences: This laptop comes with a standard two-year warranty.
The warranty has expired so the repair will cost you money.
Keep the warranty documentation somewhere safe and accessible.
words.pronunciation_tip: WOR-un-tee — three syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Don't confuse 'guarantee' with 'warranty' — warranty is specifically for products.
words.collocations: ['come with a warranty', 'one-year warranty', 'warranty has expired']

words.definition: to have enough money to buy or pay for something
example_sentences: I simply can't afford a new phone right now.
She saved diligently until she could afford the holiday.
Can you really afford to take three weeks off work?
words.pronunciation_tip: uh-FAWD — stress on second syllable.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'I have enough money for it' always — say 'I can't afford it' naturally.

words.definition: to charge someone more money than is fair or correct
example_sentences: I think they seriously overcharged me for the repair work.
Always check your bill carefully — restaurants sometimes overcharge.
We discovered we had been overcharged by 20 euros.
words.pronunciation_tip: oh-ver-CHARJ — stress on second syllable.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'they took too much money from me' — say 'they overcharged me'.

words.definition: to argue or negotiate about the price to try to pay less
example_sentences: You can easily haggle at the market but not in a supermarket.
She managed to haggle the price down by 30 percent.
Don't be afraid to haggle — the asking price is never final here.
words.pronunciation_tip: HAG-ul — two syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'I negotiated the price' for informal situations — say 'I haggled'.
words.collocations: ['haggle over the price', 'haggle at the market', 'enjoy haggling']

words.definition: to spend a lot of money on something special as a treat
example_sentences: We splurged on a luxury hotel for our anniversary.
She rarely splurges but tonight she really felt like celebrating.
This expensive meal was a real once-in-a-while splurge.
words.pronunciation_tip: SPLURJ — one syllable.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'I spent a lot of money' casually — say 'I splurged' for a deliberate treat.

words.definition: an additional charge added on top of the basic price
example_sentences: There's a 3% surcharge for paying by credit card.
A fuel surcharge was automatically added to the airline ticket.
Please note the additional surcharge for next-day delivery.
words.pronunciation_tip: SUR-charj — two syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'extra fee' every time — say 'surcharge' in more formal contexts.

words.definition: a formal document requesting payment for goods or services provided
example_sentences: Please send me a proper invoice for all the work you've done.
The invoice included every item purchased with full details.
She always pays invoices immediately on receipt.
words.pronunciation_tip: IN-voyss — two syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'bill' and 'invoice' interchangeably. Invoice is between businesses; bill is for consumers.
words.collocations: ['send an invoice', 'pay an invoice', 'issue an invoice']

words.definition: a card that earns points or rewards for regular purchases
example_sentences: I collect loyalty points on my supermarket loyalty card every week.
The loyalty card saves me a significant amount of money.
She has loyalty cards for all of her favourite regular shops.
words.pronunciation_tip: LOY-ul-tee KARD.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'points card' — say 'loyalty card'.
words.collocations: ['collect points on loyalty card', 'use a loyalty card', 'loyalty card scheme']

words.definition: a fake copy made to look exactly like the genuine original
example_sentences: The market was full of obvious counterfeit designer handbags.
She accidentally purchased a counterfeit watch at a tourist market.
Producing and selling counterfeit money is a very serious crime.
words.pronunciation_tip: KOWN-ter-fit — three syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'fake' in formal or legal contexts — say 'counterfeit'.

words.definition: a person or company that sells goods or services
example_sentences: Several vendors had set up colourful stalls at the market.
The software vendor offered a free 30-day demo version.
Always choose your vendor very carefully when making a major purchase.
words.pronunciation_tip: VEN-der — two syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'seller' in professional business contexts — say 'vendor'.

words.definition: money returned to a buyer as a reward for their purchase
example_sentences: I always use a credit card that offers cashback on purchases.
She earned significant cashback by shopping at partner stores.
The cashback promotion is only valid for this current month.
words.pronunciation_tip: KASH-bak — stress on first syllable.
words.common_mistake: Don't confuse cashback (a reward) with a refund (money back for returning something).

words.definition: (of a payment) not yet paid or settled
example_sentences: There is still a significant outstanding balance on the account.
The invoice has been outstanding and unpaid for over 30 days.
Please settle any outstanding amounts by the end of this month.
words.pronunciation_tip: owt-STAN-ding — three syllables, stress on second.
words.common_mistake: Don't confuse outstanding (excellent) with outstanding (unpaid) — context makes the meaning clear.

words.definition: a quantity of goods sent to a buyer or retailer for sale
example_sentences: The new consignment of winter shoes arrived this morning.
We are still waiting for a large consignment from the supplier.
Unfortunately the consignment was seriously damaged in transit.
words.pronunciation_tip: kun-SYN-ment — three syllables, stress on second.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'delivery of products' in business contexts — say 'consignment'.
